zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Marshall於1439年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾內瓦可時，她的父親John Marshall28歲，母親Elizabeth Emma Bingham30歲。她於大約1465年與Lawrence de Hatfield在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾內瓦可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1471年在英國瓦維其可爾維洛賀比享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Marshall于1439年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾內瓦可时，她的父亲John Marshall28岁，母亲Elizabeth Emma Bingham30岁。她于大约1465年与Lawrence de Hatfield在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾內瓦可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1471年在英国瓦維其可爾維洛賀比享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Marshall was born in 1439, in Newark, Nottinghamshire, England, her father, John Marshall, was 28 and her mother, Elizabeth Emma Bingham, was 30. She married Lawrence de Hatfield about 1465, in Newark, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1471, in Willoughby, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Marshall est née en 1439, à Newark, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John Marshall, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Emma Bingham, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Lawrence de Hatfield environ 1465, à Newark, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1471, à Willoughby, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Marshall 1439 in Newark, Nottinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Marshall 28 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Emma Bingham 30. Sie hat um 1465 in Newark, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom Lawrence de Hatfield geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1471 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Willoughby, Warwickshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Marshall è nata il nel 1439 nel Newark, Nottinghamshire, England, sua padre, John Marshall, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Emma Bingham, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Lawrence de Hatfield circa 1465, nel Newark, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1471, nel Willoughby, Warwickshire, England, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Marshallが1439年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアニューアルクで生まれた時，父John Marshallは28歳，母Elizabeth Emma Binghamは30歳であった。約1465年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアニューアルクでLawrence de Hatfieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1471年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウィロウビーで32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Marshall(이)가 1439년 영국 노틴그함실르 뉴아르크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Marshall(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Emma Bingham(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 대략 1465년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 뉴아르크에서 Lawrence de Hatfield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1471년 영국 워윅실르 윌루그h비에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Marshall nasceu em 1439, em Newark, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Marshall, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Emma Bingham, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Lawrence de Hatfield aproximadamente 1465, em Newark, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1471, em Willoughby, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Marshall родилась в 1439 г. в местности нюарк, ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Marshall было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Emma Bingham, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lawrence de Hatfield около 1465 г. в местности нюарк, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1471 г. в местности уиллоугби, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 32 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Marshall nació en 1439, en Newark, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Marshall, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Emma Bingham, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Lawrence de Hatfield aproximadamente 1465, en Newark, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1471, en Willoughby, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 32 años.
